title: Pow(x, n)
pattern: Math â€” Fast Exponentiation (Divide and Conquer)
coreIdea: Compute x raised to power n in O(log n) instead of O(n) by recursively halving the exponent, using the identity x^n = (x^(n/2))^2, adjusting for odd exponents and negative n.
recognitionCues: asks to compute a power efficiently
O(log n) expected instead of naive repeated multiplication
approach: Handle negative n by inverting x and negating n. Recursively compute half = power(x, n/2). If n is even, result is half*half. If odd, result is half*half*x. Base case: n=0 returns 1.
spaceComplexity: O(log n) recursion stack, O(1) iterative
